product_overview: SampleBook is a WhatsApp-integrated expense tracking SaaS with a React frontend, Supabase authentication, and real-time dashboard views for shared spending. It supports phone OTP login, demo mode access, group onboarding, and static marketing/support pages.
core_goals: Enable fast expense logging through WhatsApp-based interactions including text, voice, and receipt photos
Provide secure phone OTP authentication with demo mode and group-aware routing
Help users monitor personal and shared spending through admin and member dashboards
Support group creation and invite-code joining during onboarding
Present pricing, policy, and support information through public static pages
Offer a persistent dark/light theme experience across sessions

features.name: Landing Page
features.description: Public marketing homepage with product positioning, pricing, testimonials, FAQs, and navigation to login and support content.
user_flows: GET / returns the landing page with hero CTA, pricing cards, and feature sections -> User clicks the primary CTA -> GET /login returns the login page
GET / returns the landing page -> User clicks footer link for privacy or terms -> GET /privacy returns the privacy page -> GET /terms returns the terms page
GET / returns the landing page -> User submits an invalid internal navigation state or missing route -> Receive a 404-style route miss handled by the React router and remain on the public experience

features.name: Login Page
features.description: Phone-based authentication entry point with OTP verification, profile setup, demo mode login, and back-home navigation.
user_flows: GET /login returns the login form -> User submits a valid phone number -> Supabase sends OTP -> User enters 6-digit OTP -> Supabase verifies OTP -> Profile setup step collects name and gender -> User is redirected to /onboarding or /dashboard or /member based on group membership
GET /login returns the login form -> User submits an invalid phone number or incorrect OTP -> Supabase returns an authentication error -> The page displays the error and keeps the user on /login
GET /login returns the login page -> User selects demo mode -> Instant login succeeds without real phone verification -> User is redirected to the appropriate protected route

features.name: Admin Dashboard
features.description: Protected group overview for admins showing financial summaries, trend charts, category breakdowns, member progress, and group management controls.
user_flows: GET /dashboard with a valid authenticated session returns the admin dashboard -> The app loads summary cards, spending chart data, member list, and expense table from Supabase-backed state -> User views the group management panel and spending analytics
GET /dashboard without a valid session -> React Router guard redirects the user to /login -> After successful login the user is redirected back to /dashboard if they belong to an admin group

GET /dashboard with an authenticated session -> User applies an invalid filter or searches for a non-existent expense -> The expense table returns no matches and the UI shows an empty state instead of dashboard data

features.name: Member Dashboard
features.description: Protected personal dashboard for members to review their own expense summary, group ledger, and add new expenses.
user_flows: GET /member with a valid authenticated session returns the member dashboard -> The app shows personal expense summary, group ledger, and the add expense form
GET /member without a valid session -> React Router guard redirects the user to /login -> After login the app returns the user to /member if they are assigned as a member
GET /member with a valid session -> User submits an invalid add-expense form payload -> The form validation blocks submission and the UI shows an error state instead of creating an entry

features.name: Onboarding
features.description: Protected setup flow for creating a new group or joining an existing group by invite code.
user_flows: GET /onboarding with a valid authenticated session returns the onboarding screen -> User chooses create group -> The app creates the group in Supabase state -> User is routed to /dashboard or /member based on role
GET /onboarding with a valid authenticated session returns the onboarding screen -> User enters a valid invite code and chooses join group -> The invite is accepted -> User is routed to /member
GET /onboarding without a valid session -> React Router guard redirects the user to /login -> The onboarding flow cannot continue until authentication succeeds
GET /onboarding with a valid session -> User submits an invalid or expired invite code -> The app returns an error state and keeps the user on onboarding

features.name: Pricing Plans
features.description: Public pricing section describing Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers with member and usage limits plus AI OCR availability.
user_flows: GET / returns the landing page -> User reviews Free, Pro, and Enterprise pricing cards -> User clicks a CTA to start signup -> GET /login opens the authentication flow
GET / returns the landing page -> User inspects plan details -> The UI shows Free plan limits, Pro unlimited entries, and Enterprise OCR/support benefits
GET / returns the landing page -> Pricing information fails to load from the client state -> The page falls back to the static pricing content already present in the landing page layout

features.name: Static Pages
features.description: Public informational pages for privacy policy, terms of service, and support contact.
user_flows: GET /privacy returns the privacy policy page -> User reads the policy content -> User navigates back to /
GET /terms returns the terms of service page -> User reads the terms content -> User navigates to /contact for support questions
GET /contact returns the contact support form -> User submits an incomplete form -> The UI shows validation errors and does not submit the message

features.name: Theme System
features.description: Dark/light mode toggle persisted in localStorage and applied at the document root for consistent styling.
user_flows: GET / returns the app -> User toggles theme to dark mode -> The app stores samplebook_theme=dark in localStorage -> Subsequent navigation keeps the dark theme applied
GET /login returns the login page -> User toggles theme to light mode -> The app stores samplebook_theme=light in localStorage -> The UI updates immediately across the document root
GET /dashboard with an authenticated session -> localStorage does not contain a valid samplebook_theme value -> The app falls back to the default theme and keeps the page usable

features.name: Landing Page
features.description: Hero section with CTA buttons, pricing plans (Free/Pro/Enterprise), features section, testimonials, FAQ, footer with nav links (Privacy, Terms, Contact)

features.name: Login Page
features.description: Phone number input, OTP verification (6-digit), profile setup step, demo mode login, back to home navigation

features.name: Admin Dashboard
features.description: Protected route. Shows summary cards (Cash-In, Cash-Out, Net Balance, Highest Spender), spending trend chart, category donut chart, member list with progress bars, expense table, group management

features.name: Member Dashboard
features.description: Protected route. Shows personal expense summary, group ledger, add expense form

features.name: Onboarding
features.description: Protected route. Group creation or join via invite code

key_ui_elements: Glassmorphic cards with emerald green borders
Member list with avatar and progress bars
